commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes WhatsApp live QA, WhatsApp inbound group hydration, gateway diagnostics, agent/tool/model setup, provider/plugin metadata reuse, workflow env, tests, and changelog to reduce and instrument WhatsApp live RTT.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers by source inspection. The PR enables complete heap snapshot capture in the live workflow and narrows group hydration despite current main's allowFrom fallback contract.
commands.review_followup: Author or maintainer handling is needed because this protected draft has security-policy, compatibility, and live-performance proof blockers before merge.

target.title: fix(qa): reduce WhatsApp live RTT setup cost
target.body:
Summary
- Skip WhatsApp live QA agent workspace bootstrap and force exact-marker canaries to run with thinking disabled in both defaults and per-model params.
- Add per-scenario WhatsApp live timings, RSS samples, complete heap snapshot capture, and redacted gateway trace artifacts.
- Skip eager participating-group metadata hydration for DM-only WhatsApp sessions.
- Add changelog coverage for the QA and WhatsApp runtime changes.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-gateway.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/monitor-inbox.behavior.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krx90szx6z16pqh37w0206tf`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krx9ne1a4egr0qxpp22chkf5`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0
- Testbox-through-Crabbox `tbx_01krxagz91ep6d1ca29qx5b00q`: scoped `pnpm check:changed -- <touched paths>`, exit 0

Live evidence
- `26027068186` at `fdc2d0e9`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `7532ms`; trace isolated ~`5141ms` in agent/model before outbound send.
- `26027596921` at `74c4bb73`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `7930ms`; trace isolated ~`5890ms` in agent/model before outbound send.
- `26028278115` at `9ef7fbe1`: WhatsApp job passed; RTT `10393ms`; trace isolated ~`7129ms` in agent/model before outbound send.

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp live QA canary artifacts now separate channel/provider cost from agent/model cost, capture complete heap snapshots, and avoid unnecessary setup/group hydration in the DM-only path.
Real environment tested: live WhatsApp Convex QA on GitHub Actions, plus Blacksmith Testbox-through-Crabbox changed gates.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm openclaw qa whatsapp --provider-mode live-frontier --model openai/gpt-5.5 --alt-model openai/gpt-5.5 --scenario whatsapp-canary` through `qa-live-transports-convex.yml`.
Evidence after fix: WhatsApp provider-path trace is now visible; the latest run shows inbound/provider/outbound overhead is small compared with agent/model time.
Observed result after fix: this branch improves observability and removes some setup work, but it does not yet reduce the live canary into the target range; current dominant cost is agent/model pre-outbound latency.
What was not tested: full workflow green is not claimed; the exact workflow runs may show unrelated lane failures after the WhatsApp job/artifact succeeded.

commands.author: alkor2000

target.title: fix: extend CA bundle auto-injection to all 8 Node version managers
target.body:
Fixes #59494

`isNodeVersionManagerRuntime()` in `src/bootstrap/node-extra-ca-certs.ts` only detected **nvm** (via `NVM_DIR` env and `/.nvm/` path marker), but users running Node via fnm, volta, asdf, n, nodenv, nodebrew, or nvs on Linux behind corporate proxies encounter the same TLS handshake failures.

`src/daemon/service-audit.ts` already recognizes all 8 version managers (lines 302–309). This PR aligns the bootstrap layer with the daemon layer.

### Changes

- **`src/bootstrap/node-extra-ca-certs.ts`**: Extract `VERSION_MANAGER_PATH_MARKERS` array (8 markers) and use `.some()` instead of single `.includes("/.nvm/")`
- **`src/bootstrap/node-extra-ca-certs.test.ts`**: Add 7 new tests for fnm/volta/asdf/n/nodenv/nodebrew/nvs detection (15/15 passing)

---


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `isNodeVersionManagerRuntime()` (bootstrap CA cert auto-injection) only detected nvm, so fnm/volta/asdf/n/nodenv/nodebrew/nvs users on Linux behind corporate proxies hit TLS handshake failures because version-manager Node does not inherit system CA certs. This extends detection to all 8 version managers, aligned with the markers already recognized in the daemon layer.

- **Real environment tested:** Linux (Ubuntu 24.04, WSL2), Node.js 24.14.0, OpenClaw built from this PR head. Exercised the exported `isNodeVersionManagerRuntime()` and `resolveAutoNodeExtraCaCerts()` against representative version-manager execPath layouts.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** invoked the patched exported functions against each version-manager execPath via tsx:
```bash
$ node --import tsx run-check.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix:** Actual output running the patched `isNodeVersionManagerRuntime()` against each version manager's execPath (clean env, no NVM_DIR):
```
DETECTED  nvm       /home/user/.nvm/versions/node/v24.14.0/bin/node
DETECTED  fnm       /home/user/.fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin/node
DETECTED  fnm-xdg   /home/user/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.14.0/installation/bin/node
DETECTED  volta     /home/user/.volta/tools/image/node/24.14.0/bin/node
DETECTED  asdf      /home/user/.asdf/installs/nodejs/24.14.0/bin/node
DETECTED  mise      /home/user/.local/share/mise/installs/node/24.14.0/bin/node
DETECTED  n         /home/user/.n/bin/node
DETECTED  nodenv    /home/user/.nodenv/versions/24.14.0/bin/node
DETECTED  nodebrew  /home/user/.nodebrew/node/v24.14.0/bin/node
DETECTED  nvs       /home/user/nvs/node/24.14.0/x64/bin/node
DETECTED  nvs-dot   /home/user/.nvs/node/24.14.0/x64/bin/node

NVM_DIR env (backward compat)  -> true
/usr/bin/node (system, no false positive)  -> false

resolveAutoNodeExtraCaCerts (Linux + fnm) -> /etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt
```

- **Observed result after fix:** All 8 version managers are detected by execPath; the NVM_DIR env fast-path still works (backward compatible); a system Node path returns false (no false positive); and end-to-end `resolveAutoNodeExtraCaCerts` resolves the system CA bundle for an fnm runtime on Linux. Before this patch only nvm was detected; the others returned false and missed CA injection. Default fnm XDG (`~/.local/share/fnm/`) and nvs dotted-home (`~/.nvs/`) layouts are also covered.

- **What was not tested:** Live corporate-proxy TLS handshake was not reproduced (requires a proxy MITM setup); the fix targets the detection gate that precedes injection. Non-Linux platforms are skipped by design.
